_provenance.fix_note: Doc-only clarification (2026-06-17, no record changes): price.capturedAt is the /latest highTime (the instant-buy timestamp), NOT a single snapshot time for both sides. The /latest endpoint reports independent highTime and lowTime per item; price.low may be FRESHER (more recently traded) than price.high, so the low value can post-date capturedAt. Verified against data/raw/ge_latest.json: 4419 of 4420 priced records have capturedAt == highTime; the lone exception (Morrigan's javelin, item_id 22636) has a null highTime, so capturedAt falls back to lowTime. Rule: capturedAt = highTime when present, else lowTime. RE-VERIFIED (2026-06-17, no record changes): re-checked all 4420 priced records against data/raw/ge_latest.json — 4419 have capturedAt == highTime, 1 (item_id 22636) falls back to lowTime (null highTime), 0 mismatches; the highTime==capturedAt / low-may-be-fresher documentation is accurate.

known_missing: 152 mapped items have no entry in /latest (no recent trade): price.high/low/capturedAt are null
16 price ids from /latest are absent from /mapping; moved to _excluded (not joinable)
455 items missing buy_limit (null); 88 items missing high_alch/low_alch (null); honest nulls preserved

domain_stats.snapshot_note: GE prices are a volatile point-in-time snapshot from the realtime price API; high=instant-buy, low=instant-sell, in gp. GE basis applies to mains; irons cannot realize GE prices.
